channel.title: Healthcare AI Weekly
channel.description:
Deep dives into the most important healthcare AI research of the week. Each episode features in-depth analysis of two newly published peer-reviewed papers — one high-impact study and one overlooked gem — at the frontier of clinical artificial intelligence.
🎙️ Healthcare AI Weekly — The #1 podcast for healthcare AI research, clinical artificial intelligence breakthroughs, and medical AI news. Daily episodes covering machine learning in healthcare, AI diagnostics, clinical decision support, digital health innovation, health informatics, and the latest peer-reviewed clinical AI research.
Whether you're a healthcare professional, clinical researcher, AI engineer, health tech executive, medical student, or digital health innovator — Healthcare AI Weekly delivers actionable insights from the world's leading medical AI journals in under 3 minutes.
Topics include: large language models in medicine, computer vision in pathology, federated learning for patient privacy, AI-powered diagnostics, clinical NLP, radiology AI, drug discovery AI, ambient clinical intelligence, and healthcare workflow automation.
Created by Raphael T. Malikian, MBBS, BSc (Hons) — with over 20 years of healthcare domain experience including working as a Family Medicine Physician and bootstrapping a direct primary care startup, bridging the gap between cutting-edge AI research and real-world clinical impact.
In true AI fashion, this podcast was created with AI tools including text-to-speech using Microsoft Edge TTS and Hermes Agent by Nous Research. All research content is based on peer-reviewed publications.
For comments, questions, or concerns: rtmalikian@gmail.com YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@RaphaelMalikian-g4h
Subscribe wherever you listen to podcasts — Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Amazon Music, Google Podcasts, and more.
⚕️ MEDICAL DISCLAIMER: This podcast is for educational and informational purposes only. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any medical condition, and should not be relied upon as medical advice. The content discussed reflects published research and does not constitute clinical recommendations. If you have any health concerns or medical questions, please consult a licensed healthcare professional immediately. Never disregard professional medical advice or delay seeking it because of something you have heard on this podcast.
channel.copyright: © 2026 Healthcare AI Weekly | Created by Raphael T. Malikian
channel.ns0:author: Healthcare AI Weekly
channel.ns0:summary:
Deep dives into the most important healthcare AI research of the week. Each episode features in-depth analysis of two newly published peer-reviewed papers — one high-impact study and one overlooked gem — at the frontier of clinical artificial intelligence.
🎙️ Healthcare AI Weekly — The #1 podcast for healthcare AI research, clinical artificial intelligence breakthroughs, and medical AI news. Daily episodes covering machine learning in healthcare, AI diagnostics, clinical decision support, digital health innovation, health informatics, and the latest peer-reviewed clinical AI research.
Whether you're a healthcare professional, clinical researcher, AI engineer, health tech executive, medical student, or digital health innovator — Healthcare AI Weekly delivers actionable insights from the world's leading medical AI journals in under 3 minutes.
Topics include: large language models in medicine, computer vision in pathology, federated learning for patient privacy, AI-powered diagnostics, clinical NLP, radiology AI, drug discovery AI, ambient clinical intelligence, and healthcare workflow automation.
Created by Raphael T. Malikian, MBBS, BSc (Hons) — with over 20 years of healthcare domain experience including working as a Family Medicine Physician and bootstrapping a direct primary care startup, bridging the gap between cutting-edge AI research and real-world clinical impact.
In true AI fashion, this podcast was created with AI tools including text-to-speech using Microsoft Edge TTS and Hermes Agent by Nous Research. All research content is based on peer-reviewed publications.
For comments, questions, or concerns: rtmalikian@gmail.com YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@RaphaelMalikian-g4h
Subscribe wherever you listen to podcasts — Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Amazon Music, Google Podcasts, and more.
⚕️ MEDICAL DISCLAIMER: This podcast is for educational and informational purposes only. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any medical condition, and should not be relied upon as medical advice. The content discussed reflects published research and does not constitute clinical recommendations. If you have any health concerns or medical questions, please consult a licensed healthcare professional immediately. Never disregard professional medical advice or delay seeking it because of something you have heard on this podcast.

item.title: Healthcare AI Weekly: AI Triage Meets Governance | Healthcare AI Weekly
item.description:
I’m Raphael Malikian, a healthcare AI builder with 20+ years of domain experience, from family medicine practice to bootstrapping a direct primary care startup. This channel documents my healthcare-AI pivot: building in public, sharing insights on clinical workflows, digital health, LLM evaluation, automation, research ops, and turning real projects into a practical portfolio.
Every week I release Healthcare AI Weekly which is a journal club discussing an important peer-reviewed healthcare AI article, and one you might have missed.
Do you have a healthcare problem that you help with solving? Reach out. My email is rtmalikian@gmail.com
Links
Github github.com/rtmalikian
LinkedIn linkedin.com/in/raphael-t-malikian-mbbs-bsc-hons-71075436a
---
This week in Healthcare AI Weekly: a source-grounded look at two new peer-reviewed healthcare AI papers.
1) Bavali-Gazik et al. — Developing and validating an AI-based electronic triage model for cardiac-suspected ED patients. PMID: 42182049; PMCID: PMC13195029. 2) Hiratsuka et al. — AI/ML in Alaska Native healthcare systems: symposium perspectives. PMID: 42176020; PMCID: PMC13202658.
Comment question: which layer matters most for healthcare AI right now — model performance, workflow fit, or governance?
Synthetic voice disclosure: narration uses edge-tts en-US-AndrewNeural synthetic voice for review production.
Watch this episode on YouTube: https://youtu.be/Sv_W4LZsfAI
YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@RaphaelMalikian-g4h
Created by Raphael T. Malikian (rtmalikian@gmail.com). In true AI fashion, this podcast was created with AI tools including text-to-speech using Microsoft Edge TTS and Hermes Agent by Nous Research.
#HealthcareAI #ClinicalAI #MedicalAI #AIinMedicine #DigitalHealth #MachineLearning #HealthTech #AIResearch #ClinicalDecisionSupport #HealthcareInnovation
⚕️ MEDICAL DISCLAIMER: This podcast is for educational and informational purposes only. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any medical condition, and should not be relied upon as medical advice. The content discussed reflects published research and does not constitute clinical recommendations. If you have any health concerns or medical questions, please consult a licensed healthcare professional immediately. Never disregard professional medical advice or delay seeking it because of something you have heard on this podcast.

I’m Raphael Malikian, a healthcare AI builder with 20+ years of domain experience, from family medicine practice to bootstrapping a direct primary care startup. This channel documents my healthcare-AI pivot: building in public, sharing insights on clinical workflows, digital health, LLM evaluation, automation, research ops, and turning real projects into a practical portfolio.
Every week I release Healthcare AI Weekly which is a journal club discussing an important peer-reviewed healthcare AI article, and one you might have missed.

This week in Healthcare AI Weekly: a source-grounded look at two new peer-reviewed healthcare AI papers.
1) Bavali-Gazik et al. — Developing and validating an AI-based electronic triage model for cardiac-suspected ED patients. PMID: 42182049; PMCID: PMC13195029. 2) Hiratsuka et al. — AI/ML in Alaska Native healthcare systems: symposium perspectives. PMID: 42176020; PMCID: PMC13202658.
